In fact, NDT results are not only exploitable for verifying the structural integrity of RC elements, but they are also essential for guiding any further (and maybe invasive) tests. This data was then integrated into the Live Information System (LIS) platform, which is a comprehensive multidomain monitoring system, to create a live, continuously updatable digital twin of the structure, enabling long-term structural health monitoring, failure prediction, and maintenance planning. Through minimally invasive and repeatable NDTs, the procedure establishes a baseline for detecting future structural deterioration from a sustainable perspective.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种将无损检测数据集成到建筑信息模型中的方法，用于监测既有钢筋混凝土建筑的健康状态。通过意大利卡梅里诺的案例研究，利用无损检测快速获取材料特性并优化检测计划，最终在实时信息系统中构建可持续更新的数字孪生体，实现长期结构健康监测与维护规划。

papers.title: From Propagation to Prediction: Point-level Uncertainty Evaluation of MLS Point Clouds under Limited Ground Truth
papers.abstract: Abstract. Evaluating uncertainty is critical for reliable use of Mobile Laser Scanning (MLS) point clouds in many high-precision applications such as Scan-to-BIM, deformation analysis, and 3D modeling. However, obtaining the ground truth (GT) for evaluation is often costly and infeasible in many real-world applications. To reduce this long-standing reliance on GT in uncertainty evaluation research, this study presents a learning-based framework for MLS point clouds that integrates optimal neighborhood estimation with geometric feature extraction. Experiments on a real-world dataset show that the proposed framework is feasible and the XGBoost model delivers fully comparable accuracy to Random Forest while achieving substantially higher efficiency (about 3 times faster), providing initial evidence that geometric features can be used to predict point-level uncertainty quantified by the C2C distance. In summary, this study shows that MLS point clouds’ uncertainty is learnable, offering a novel learning-based viewpoint towards uncertainty evaluation research.
papers.venue: ISPRS annals of the photogrammetry, remote sensing and spatial information sciences
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种基于学习的移动激光扫描点云不确定性评估框架，通过集成最优邻域估计与几何特征提取，在无需完整地面真值的情况下预测点级不确定性。实验表明，XGBoost模型在精度上与随机森林相当，但效率提升约3倍，验证了几何特征可用于预测C2C距离量化的不确定性。

papers.title: Integrating Digital Tools for Automated Circularity Assessment of Construction Products: A Case Study
papers.abstract: The circular economy is recognised as a key topic that requires the development of user-friendly methodologies for circularity assessment, with digitalisation supporting more accurate evaluation processes. This study proposes an automated digital tool to calculate products’ Circularity Level (LC), defined in UNI/TS 11820:2024 and aligned with ISO/TC 323 circular economy standards (ISO 59004, ISO 59010, and ISO 59020) and the Level(s) EU sustainability framework. Specifically, an Excel-based calculator is developed to encode regulatory requirements, automatically compute LC values, generate radar charts highlighting improvement areas, and export results to MS Word for automated stakeholder reporting. Additionally, for construction materials, an information flow between MS Excel and Autodesk Revit is established using Dynamo, enabling the automated creation of product-related BIM objects and the integration of circularity data into the BIM model. The workflow is demonstrated through its application to a single case, namely the ITER Project, which implements earthen plasters enhanced by by-products from the agricultural and stone supply chains. An LC of 43.77% is obtained, driven by material efficiency and recovery, but limited by renewable energy use and end-of-life management. Future research will investigate AI techniques to optimise indicator scores and enhance digital circularity assessment in the construction sector.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种基于UNI/TS 11820:2024标准的自动化数字工具，通过Excel编码和Dynamo实现建筑产品循环性水平（LC）的自动计算及BIM集成。以ITER项目案例评估掺入副产物的地基灰泥，得到LC值43.77%，揭示了材料效率优势与可再生能源及末端管理的局限。

papers.title: Real-Time Settlement of Civil Construction Works through 5D/6D BIM–IoT Convergence: A Verifiable Quantitative Engineering Framework for Digital Cost Closure in Peru
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对秘鲁土木工程，提出一种通过5D/6D BIM与物联网融合实现实时沉降监测的框架，旨在为数字成本结算提供可验证的定量工程方法。

papers.title: Development and Integration of LADM and BIM for 3D Cadastre in Iran
papers.abstract: The rapid urbanization and development of vertical construction and subsurface spaces in Iran, along with national policy emphasis on transparency in land administration, have highlighted the need to transition from a two-dimensional (2D) to a three-dimensional (3D) cadastre. Iran’s current cadastral system, based on 2D parcel maps, fails to adequately capture complex ownership relationships in multi-story buildings and underground spaces. This study proposes an integrated model combining the Land Administration Domain Model (LADM) country profile with the Industry Foundation Classes (IFC) standard for 3D cadastre implementation. The LADM country profile of Iran was extended to support 3D spatial units. A 3D building model was developed in Autodesk Revit by integrating as-built cadastral maps with architectural drawings and exported in IFC format. Legal attributes were added to Building Information Modeling (BIM) elements in Simplebim via custom Property Sets, allowing physical and legal information to be stored in one IFC file. Functional and interoperability testing in IFC-compatible software showed that the file could be read and used for legal-spatial queries, although the results may depend on each software’s IFC support. For national use, database-level implementation may provide a more reliable option. These results demonstrate that integrating LADM and BIM for a 3D cadastre is technically feasible in Iran at the building scale, as shown by the successful mapping between LADM classes and IFC entities, implementation of custom Property Sets, and interoperability testing across IFC-compatible software platforms.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对伊朗城市化与土地管理需求，将LADM国家扩展与IFC标准集成，通过Revit建模并导出IFC文件，在Simplebim中添加法律属性，实现物理与法律信息统一存储。测试表明该方法在建筑尺度上可行，数据库级实施更适用于国家应用。

papers.title: A BIM-interoperable optimisation plug-in for structural design of whole-roof timber truss systems
papers.abstract: Abstract Optimal design of timber roof truss topology, geometry, and cross-sections is limited in practice due to the absence of integrated, code-aware design tools. This paper presents a Grasshopper 3D-based optimisation plug-in. The plug-in combines site-specific action generation with Eurocode 1, finite element analysis in Karamba3D, Eurocode 5 member checks in Python and genetic algorithm (GA)-based topology and geometry search within a single parametric workflow. Considering three truss types Fink, Pratt, and Howe cross-section sizing is performed against a discrete library of solid timber sections. Optimised models export to Industry Foundation Class format, supporting interoperability with building information modelling based coordination tools. The plug-in is applied across six Norwegian locations, as well as four inventory scenarios consisting of one new and three reclaimed component stocks. This results in location-specific, minimum mass roof configurations that satisfy Eurocode ultimate limit states. Cross-section optimisation with Eurocode 5 compliance checks executes in under 20 s on a standard office computer. The overall computational cost is dominated by the GA, with total runtimes varying according to truss type and stock composition, ranging from a minimum of 1 h 15 min to a maximum of 5 h 54 min. The applicability of the plug-in is constrained by predefined geometric limits, including a roof pitch between 20 <mml:math xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" overflow="scroll"> <mml:mrow> <mml:msup> <mml:mi/> <mml:mrow> <mml:mo>∘</mml:mo> </mml:mrow> </mml:msup> </mml:mrow> </mml:math> and 35 <mml:math xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" overflow="scroll"> <mml:mrow> <mml:msup> <mml:mi/> <mml:mrow> <mml:mo>∘</mml:mo> </mml:mrow> </mml:msup> </mml:mrow> </mml:math> , bay spacing from 1.55 m to 2.8 m, and web member spacing between 0.7 m and 1.25 m. Cost and embodied carbon (EC) assessments show that minimising mass alone is an insufficient objective when connection contributions are included. Plug-in extensions to broader geometry, more locations, direct cost and EC optimisation, and reliability-based design for reclaimed stock are identified as priorities for future work.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种基于Grasshopper 3D的优化插件，整合场地特定荷载生成、欧洲规范有限元分析和遗传算法拓扑搜索，用于木屋顶桁架结构设计。该插件支持导出工业基础类格式，实现与建筑信息模型协调工具的互操作性，并在挪威六地点和四种库存场景中验证了满足欧洲规范极限状态的最小质量屋顶配置。

papers.title: A review on BIM’s contribution to an efficient RFI management in complex geotechnical and construction projects
papers.abstract: Abstract Requests for Information (RFIs) are essential for technical communication and decision-making in construction projects, yet their management remains fragmented and inefficient, particularly in complex geotechnical contexts. This study examines how Building Information Modeling (BIM) and GeoBIM environments can support more structured and collaborative RFI workflows. A qualitative and exploratory approach was adopted, combining a systematic literature review, analysis of international standards, and examination of representative case studies with different BIM maturity levels. The analysis identifies persistent challenges related to interoperability, workflow standardization, and the integration of geotechnical information in digital environments. In response to these gaps, a conceptual BIM-based RFI management framework is proposed, introducing the CUBO-RFI as a parametric three-dimensional object to enhance traceability, visualization, and coordination. Although not presented as an empirically validated solution, the framework illustrates the potential of model-based approaches to improve communication, monitoring, and lifecycle tracking of technical queries. The findings further highlight the role of BIM and GeoBIM as enabling technologies for integrated information flows and more informed decision-making in complex projects. This study contributes to the digital transformation of the AECO sector by offering a structured conceptual model that supports future empirical validation and practical implementation.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 通过系统文献综述、国际标准分析与案例研究，探讨了建筑信息模型（BIM）与GeoBIM环境如何支持复杂岩土与施工项目中请求信息（RFI）的结构化管理；提出了基于BIM的RFI管理概念框架，引入CUBO-RFI参数化三维对象以增强可追溯性与可视化协调。

papers.title: SemanVIEW: A Modular Web Platform for Semantic BIM Integration and Visualization in Construction
papers.abstract: This study introduces SemanVIEW, a modular platform designed to enhance semantic BIM workflows by integrating semantic parsing, semantic enrichment, and interactive 3D visualization. Traditional BIM systems often remain limited to geometric representations, while semantic approaches aim to capture meaning, relationships, and contextual attributes. SemanVIEW addresses this gap by operationalizing enrichment through a semantic dictionary, ensuring IFC–ontology integration, and embedding real time querying and compliance validation. The platform was evaluated through case study testing, focusing on accuracy, coverage, efficiency, and usability. Results demonstrated high accuracy in semantic mapping, broad coverage of contextual enrichment, efficient performance with IFC files processed in under five seconds, and positive usability feedback from test users. These findings confirm that SemanVIEW not only consolidates theoretical advances but also delivers practical, reproducible outcomes for semantic BIM workflows. By bridging enrichment, querying, and visualization within a unified environment, SemanVIEW contributes to ongoing developments in semantic BIM and lays the foundation for interoperable digital twin applications. The study highlights its potential to improve traceability, compliance, and decision support, while pointing toward future directions in ontology expansion, BIM–GIS–IoT integration, and large scale validation.
papers.venue: Gazi University Journal of Science Part A Engineering and Innovation
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出SemanVIEW模块化平台，集成语义解析、丰富与交互式3D可视化，解决传统BIM几何表达局限。利用语义词典实现IFC-本体集成，支持实时查询与合规验证，测试显示语义映射准确、IFC处理高效（<5秒），为语义BIM与数字孪生提供可复现方案。

papers.title: A simulation-driven digital twin framework for parametric design of wing corner structures in traditional Chinese architecture using Dynamo
papers.abstract: Heritage conservation of traditional Chinese timber-framed architecture faces a dual challenge: The imminent loss of craftsmanship knowledge transmitted through master–apprentice traditions, and the progressive degradation and deformation of wooden components caused by environmental exposure and repeated restoration interventions. Among its key structural elements, the wing corner is one of the most iconic assemblies in traditional timber architecture. It exhibits complex geometric characteristics, including bi-directional curvature, intricate mortise-and-tenon joints, and significant regional variations between northern and southern building traditions. Existing Scan-to-BIM approaches can only generate noneditable static mesh models, which are insufficient for renovation simulation and structural performance assessment. Parametric modeling techniques, which encode construction rules from historical treatises into computational representations, provide a promising alternative. This study proposes a component-level parametric framework implemented in Dynamo, establishing a parameter-driven four-stage modeling workflow to generate two typologically distinct wing corner forms: The rigid northern official style governed by the Yingzao Fashi, and the flexible southern garden style governed by the Yingzao Fayuan. Validation using case studies of the Hall of Supreme Harmony and the Dianchun Yi Pavilion demonstrates that the proposed framework reproduces wing corner geometry with sub-centimeter accuracy, providing a reusable technical approach for HBIM-based digital reconstruction of historic timber architecture.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种基于Dynamo的仿真驱动数字孪生框架，通过参数化建模工作流生成北方官式与南方园林两种翼角形式，并以太和殿与殿春簃为案例验证，实现了亚厘米级几何精度，为历史木构建筑的HBIM数字重建提供了可复用的技术路径。

papers.title: COMPARATIVE STUDY OF BIM ELECTRONIC REVIEW IN JAPAN AND FOREIGN COUNTRIES
papers.abstract: This study compares BIM-enabled electronic permitting and model/data checking across seven foreign cases (Singapore, Norway, Korea, Australia, Estonia, New Zealand, and the UK) using a three-layer framework: institutional design, operational practice, and technical implementation. Cross-case analysis identifies four implementation archetypes, ranging from mandatory IFC-based submissions with rule-based automated compliance checking to early-stage pilot programs. Based on the typology, we identify foundational conditions for institutional design: traceable submission management, automated data-quality validation at submission, and a clearly bounded scope of automation with defined accountability.
papers.venue: Journal of Architecture and Planning (Transactions of AIJ)
papers.ai_summary_zh: 通过构建制度设计、操作实践和技术实施三层框架，比较了新加坡、挪威等七国的BIM电子审查案例，识别出从强制性IFC提交与规则自动化合规检查到早期试点项目的四种实施类型，并提出了可追溯的提交管理、自动数据质量验证及明确自动化范围与责任等制度设计基础条件。

papers.title: Огляд сучасних методів обробки хмар точок для документування зруйнованих та історичних будівель
papers.abstract: У статті узагальнено сучасні підходи до швидкого документування та оцінювання стану зруйнованих і історичних будівель на основі 3D-хмар точок. Розглянуто комбінований збір даних (наземне лазерне сканування, фотограмметрія, аерознімання з безпілотних літальних апаратів (БПЛА), Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS)-прив’язка) та типові етапи Scan-to-BIM/Scan-to-CAD конвеєрів: попередню обробку, виділення геометричних примітивів (Random Sample Consensus, RANSAC), оцінювання орієнтацій і нормалей (Principal Component Analysis, PCA), кластеризацію/сегментацію, а також семантичну сегментацію на основі глибинного навчання. Окрему увагу приділено моделям і алгоритмам, що зберігають фактичні деформації (неортогональні кути, нахили, прогини) як важливі ознаки технічного стану та культурної автентичності. Проаналізовано можливості наявних інструментів і обмеження автоматизації, а також окреслено ключові прогалини: нестача еталонних наборів даних і метрик, масштабованість, стійкість до шуму/перекриттів і семантична насиченість моделей. Наведено перспективні напрями розвитку деформаційно-усвідомлених робочих процесів для планування відновлення та реставрації.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 该论文综述了基于3D点云快速记录受损和历史建筑状态的现代方法，重点分析了结合地面激光扫描、摄影测量和无人机数据的Scan-to-BIM流程，并探讨了保留实际变形特征以支持修复规划的自动化工具与未来方向。

papers.title: Toward agent-native infrastructure in AEC: From fragmented knowledge to executable workflows
papers.abstract: The architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) sector has achieved substantial digitalization through building information models, common data environments, digital twins, platforms, and large language model-based assistants. Yet project knowledge remains fragmented across data structures, software systems, organizations, and lifecycle stages, limiting its reuse, governance, and translation into coordinated action. This paper argues that the next bottleneck in AEC is not the lack of digital tools but the lack of an infrastructure capable of transforming fragmented project artifacts into governed and executable knowledge assets. Drawing on a critical synthesis of research on AEC fragmentation, digitalization, knowledge governance, and autonomous agents, the paper introduces the concept of agent-native infrastructure in AEC. It defines such infrastructure as a governable socio-technical architecture that couples semantic knowledge organization, intelligence mediation, tool interaction, and policy-constrained workflow execution. The paper reframes AEC digitalization as an infrastructure problem centered on knowledge executability, explains why existing pathways focused on connection, representation, and visibility have not yet produced governable execution at scale, and outlines a research agenda on technical development, human-AI collaborative systems, organizational knowledge management, and knowledge co-production between research and practice.
papers.venue: Journal of Construction Engineering Management & Innovation
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对建筑、工程与施工领域知识碎片化问题，通过批判性综合研究，提出“面向智能体的基础设施”概念，将其定义为一种可治理的社会技术架构，整合语义知识组织、智能中介、工具交互与策略约束的工作流执行，以将碎片化项目制品转化为可执行的知识资产。

papers.title: Review of the Effects of Digital Transformation on the Construction Industry
papers.abstract: Digital transformation (DT) is increasingly reshaping construction through data-driven design, connected assets, and platform-based coordination across fragmented project ecosystems. This paper provides a systematic review of DT research in the construction industry using a PRISMA-guided search strategy in Scopus (2016–2025). After de-duplication and multi-stage screening (title/metadata, abstract, and full-text eligibility), 30 peer‑reviewed studies were retained for analysis. We apply an inductive conceptual synthesis to develop two interlinked taxonomies: (i) DT dimensions in project-based settings (digital strategy, stakeholder engagement and coordination, innovation culture, enabling technologies, and data & analytics) and (ii) construction-industry context dimensions (stakeholders, project phases, construction types, key processes/techniques, and economic–environmental considerations). Cross-study synthesis shows strong concentration on BIM/digital twins, IoT-enabled monitoring and automation, and AI/analytics for safety, quality and scheduling; by contrast, organizational change mechanisms, interoperability governance, and long-term outcome evaluation remain underdeveloped. The review distinguishes technologies that are potentially transformative (integrated BIM–twin platforms and analytics-enabled decision infrastructures) from tools that primarily yield incremental gains (standalone automation/sensing). We conclude with actionable implications for researchers (socio-technical and cross-context validation) and practitioners (capability building, data governance, and phased integration roadmaps).
papers.ai_summary_zh: 基于PRISMA框架对2016-2025年30篇文献进行系统综述，通过归纳概念综合构建了数字化转型维度与建筑行业情境维度的双分类体系。研究发现BIM/数字孪生、物联网监控与AI分析是当前研究热点，但组织变革机制与互操作性治理仍待深化。

papers.title: A review of microwave radar technology for monitoring bridges and other civil structures: Applications, challenges, and prospects
papers.abstract: Microwave radar technology, owing to its non-contact, high-precision, and all-weather monitoring capabilities, has gradually emerged as an important tool for the structural health monitoring (SHM) of bridges and other civil structures. This review systematically summarizes recent research advances and engineering applications of microwave radar in structural safety assessment, dynamic response identification, and long-term deformation monitoring. First, from the perspectives of measurement principles and system classification, the performance characteristics and applicability of frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW), stepped-frequency continuous-wave (SFCW), synthetic aperture radar (SAR), and multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) radar in different monitoring scenarios are discussed. Particular attention is then given to key applications in bridge engineering, including environmental interference suppression, synchronous multi-target measurement, cable-force and deflection identification, as well as modal parameter extraction and damage diagnosis. Existing studies indicate that microwave radar can achieve high accuracy and stability in the non-contact monitoring of bridges. Nevertheless, several technical bottlenecks remain, particularly in interference resistance under extreme environmental conditions, signal aliasing mitigation, and the identification of subtle damage. On this basis, the review further examines the expanded application of microwave radar in complex structures such as high-rise buildings, dams, slopes, and heritage buildings, highlighting its engineering value in deformation monitoring and disaster early warning. Finally, future research directions are outlined, including the development of multi-frequency fusion and adaptive compensation techniques; intelligent identification algorithms informed by structural dynamics; deeper integration of radar monitoring with existing SHM systems, BIM/digital twin frameworks, and operation and maintenance platforms; and new radar architectures that are low-cost, easy to deploy, and suitable for urban complexes and lifeline infrastructure.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 该综述系统梳理了微波雷达技术在桥梁等土木结构健康监测中的研究进展与工程应用，重点分析了FMCW、SAR等雷达系统在动态响应识别、模态参数提取及损伤诊断中的性能与挑战，并探讨了其与BIM、数字孪生结合的未来发展方向。

papers.title: DIGITAL-DRIVEN RECONSTRUCTION OF UKRAINE: CONSTRUCTION INNOVATION, MIGRATION AND ECONOMIC RECOVERY
papers.abstract: The study is devoted to digitally controlled reconstruction of Ukraine as a strategic mechanism for accelerating reconstruction and economic recovery in the context of migration-induced labour shortages in construction. The aim of the article is to determine the impact of digital innovations (BIM, digital twins, AI planning, e-permits, modular construction and digital procurement) on increasing the productivity of the industry, compensating for staff losses and strengthening the investment capacity of reconstruction. The methodology includes systematisation of scientific approaches to the reconstruction economy, analysis of modern digital solutions in the construction sector, and a comparative review of international practices in post-crisis reconstruction. Key findings show that the digitalisation of construction processes increases productivity, partially compensates for labour shortages, reduces time and cost losses in projects, technological productivity gains partially offset labour shortages, reduce corruption risks and strengthen donor confidence, which contributes to increased investment and accelerated economic recovery in Ukraine.
papers.venue: AGORA INTERNATIONAL JOURNAL OF ECONOMICAL SCIENCES
papers.ai_summary_zh: 该研究探讨数字化驱动的乌克兰重建战略，通过分析BIM、数字孪生等创新技术对建筑业生产率的影响，评估其在缓解劳动力短缺、降低腐败风险及加速经济复苏中的作用。

papers.title: A Literature Review on the Application of Physical AI Robotics for Zero-Energy Building Performance Gap
papers.abstract: Zero-energy buildings (ZEBs) exhibit energy performance gaps (EPGs) of +14-+30% between design predictions and actual operations.This study reviewed 27 publications (19 peer-reviewed articles and eight technical/industrial reports, 2022-2025) across three domains, physical AI robotics, physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), and building digital twins, to analyze the applicability of emerging physical AI technologies to ZEB performance gap closure.Existing reviews on physics-informed machine learning (PIML) focus on methodological taxonomy without addressing robotics or ZEB performance gaps, whereas ZEB digital twin studies rely on fixed Internet of Things (IoT) sensors without autonomous robotics and BIM-robot integration studies are limited to indoor environment monitoring without energy optimization linkages.This review reveals that no prior study has integrated these domains within a unified ZEB performance management framework.Based on the review findings, a five-stage closed-loop framework, zero energy building-physical AI robotics (ZEB-PAR), is derived: Perceive, Diagnose, Update, Optimize, and Verify.Technology readiness level (TRL) analysis revealed an asymmetry between the Perceive-Verify axis (mean TRL 7) and the Diagnose-Optimize axis (mean TRL 5), indicating that robotic data collection and re-inspection are nearly deployment-ready, whereas physics-informed AI and LLM-based optimization require further building-domain adaptation.
papers.venue: Journal of the Korean Solar Energy Society
papers.ai_summary_zh: 该研究通过综述27篇2022-2025年的文献，分析了物理AI机器人、物理信息神经网络与建筑数字孪生三大领域在零能耗建筑性能差距闭合中的应用，并提出了五阶段闭环框架ZEB-PAR，揭示了感知-验证轴与诊断-优化轴之间的技术成熟度不对称性。

papers.title: BIM-Based Building Permit
papers.abstract: The development of a Building Information Modelling (BIM)-based building permit process in Estonia is a direct response to challenges within the existing legal framework for building permits—namely, fragmented regulations and subjective interpretation, which hinder consistent compliance and slow down the permitting process. To address these issues, the Ministry of Climate has initiated the integration of BIM into Estonian Building Registry, aiming to improve regulatory clarity, automate compliance checks, and streamline permit issuance within the architecture, engineering, and construction sector. This innovative approach leverages BIM models in the Industry Foundation Classes format, enabling digital permit submissions without the need for conversion into traditional formats. The result is a faster, more transparent process supported by a web-based platform and a simplified user interface. The system is centred on an automated rule-checking engine that verifies compliance with 47 regulatory criteria, prioritised via an impact-effort matrix to maximise processing efficiency, minimise human error, and limit IT development effort. These checks include housing requirements, fire safety, and accessibility. To enable this, the project emphasises the standardisation of digital requirements and the alignment of legislation with structured data practices, paving the way for effective automation. Standardised BIM data improves consistency across projects and enhances interoperability with Estonia&s;s National Digital Twin, which allows 3D visualisation of planned buildings in their surrounding environment—supporting better-informed decisions by all stakeholders. By reducing data redundancy, minimising human error, and automating parts of the review process, this semi-automated system not only shortens processing times but also supports increased productivity, transparency, and alignment with Estonia&s;s broader digitalisation goals in the construction sector.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 本研究针对爱沙尼亚建筑许可流程中法规碎片化与主观解读问题，提出基于BIM的数字化审批方法。通过采用IFC格式的BIM模型与自动化规则检查引擎，系统可验证47项合规标准（含消防与无障碍要求），并整合至国家数字孪生平台，实现审批效率提升与人为错误减少。

papers.title: Using Information Delivery Specification for Compliance Checking in Building Permitting
papers.abstract: The digitalisation of building permitting processes has a significant potential to improve efficiency, transparency, and accuracy. One of the key components of building permitting digitalisation is Automated Compliance Checking (ACC). However, transforming regulatory requirements into machine-readable formats to enable ACC processes remains a challenge due to the complexity and variability of legal texts. This chapter examines the application of the Information Delivery Specification (IDS), an open standard from buildingSMART International, as a bridge between regulatory requirements and Building Information Modelling (BIM)-based ACC. IDS enables the definition of information requirements for BIM models in a standardised, machine-readable format, which facilitates both information availability checks and some logical dependencies. While IDS supports formal and logical checks, limitations remain in some cases. These include handling exceptions, complex dependencies, and spatial checks. Two implementation cases, drawn from the City of Vienna and Nepal, are presented to illustrate practical, real-world applications of the proposed approach. The chapter also discusses the importance of combining IDS with complementary tools and rule-based methodologies to achieve comprehensive ACC process in building permitting. Interdisciplinary collaboration among legal, domain, and Information Technology experts is essential to develop accurate, interoperable, and scalable digital permitting solutions. Overall, IDS represents a promising step towards harmonised, efficient, and transparent building permitting processes, with potential for further enhancement through extensions and integration with openBIM standards.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 研究采用建筑智慧国际开放标准信息交付规范（IDS），作为连接法规要求与BIM自动化合规检查的桥梁，通过维也纳和尼泊尔的案例验证了其在标准化信息需求定义和逻辑检查中的有效性，但指出其在处理例外、复杂依赖和空间检查方面存在局限。

papers.title: Systematization and completeness assessment of source data in the life cycle management of a capital construction facility (the case of an apartment building)
papers.abstract: Source data of a capital construction facility is generated at the early stages of the life cycle and must be transferred between stages without loss; its completeness, reliability and machine-readability largely determine the efficiency of the facility life cycle management. Information gaps are most acute at the handover of the facility to the longest and most resource-intensive stage of the life cycle — operation, which accounts for about 75 % of total costs. In domestic practice the composition, collection methods and form of source data are not systematized or differentiated by object type and by the availability of an information model, which causes information gaps in the life cycle. The aim is to identify and systematize the features of collection and completeness assessment of source data in the life cycle management of a facility, primarily an apartment building. Using apartment buildings as an example, the composition of source data is systematized into five functional groups detailed by attribute subgroups, carriers, update frequency and responsible parties; a comparative analysis of two collection strategies — primary (inspection, laser scanning, scan-to-BIM) and model-based with COBie exchange — is carried out against seven criteria; a data collection and verification procedure and an attribute completeness coefficient are proposed. It is established that the efficiency of the facility life cycle management is determined not by the volume but by the structuring of source data and the continuity of its transfer between stages; a differentiated collection approach depending on object type and model availability and a quantitative data completeness tool are proposed.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 以公寓建筑为例，将源数据系统化为五个功能组，通过属性子组、载体、更新频率和责任人细化；对比扫描到BIM与COBie交换策略，提出数据采集验证流程和属性完整性系数。研究发现管理效率取决于数据结构化与传递连续性，据此提出差异化采集方法与定量完整性评估工具。

papers.title: AR, VR and XR in Building Permitting
papers.abstract: The adoption of the openBIM approval process enables the use of new technologies that were limited or impossible to implement with previous 2D submissions. Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR) and Extended Reality (XR) are among these technologies, and the openBIM approval process opens up new applications within the authority. Approval processes traditionally based on two-dimensional plans often face difficulties in communicating between experts and non-experts. AR, VR or XR add value by providing three-dimensional visualisations of construction projects that enhance spatial understanding and improve transparency for all stakeholders. The visualisation of proposed buildings in an urban context helps citizens to better understand building projects, reduces potential objections and thus shortens the approval process. Extended application scenarios include assisting authorities in monitoring construction sites. This chapter provides an overview of current use cases, challenges and future prospects for the use of AR, VR and XR in openBIM building permits and highlights the potential of these technologies to transform public administration processes.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 本研究探讨了增强现实（AR）、虚拟现实（VR）和扩展现实（XR）在基于openBIM的建筑审批流程中的应用。通过三维可视化技术提升空间理解与审批透明度，这些技术有助于改善专家与非专家间的沟通，减少公众异议并缩短审批周期，同时为施工监管提供新可能。

papers.title: Integrating Life Cycle Assessment and Circular Economy Approaches for Sustainable Building: A Comprehensive Review
papers.abstract: The construction sector is a major contributor to resource depletion and environmental emissions, necessitating the transition toward Circular Economy (CE) strategies aligned with Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) is widely applied to evaluate environmental performance; however, conventional approaches often lack the dynamic, digital, and multi-scale capabilities required to assess circular systems effectively. This study systematically reviews 61 peer-reviewed articles published between 2019 and 2025 to examine the integration of CE and LCA in the building sector. The selected studies were classified based on LCA methodology, CE strategy, system boundary, scale of application, and use of digital tools. A critical thematic synthesis reveals a growing shift toward dynamic, parametric, and hybrid LCA approaches, enabling improved representation of reuse cycles, material recovery, and lifetime extension. However, the findings also demonstrate that results are highly sensitive to methodological assumptions, particularly system boundaries, allocation rules, and service life parameters, which are often insufficiently validated. While CE strategies such as reuse, recycling, bio-based materials, and design for disassembly generally show environmental benefits, their effectiveness is context-dependent and may be overstated under idealized scenarios. Digital tools, including BIM, digital twins, and AI-based models, enhance data traceability and scenario analysis but remain constrained by interoperability challenges, data limitations, and limited large-scale implementation. Overall, the review identifies a gap between methodological advancement and practical robustness, highlighting the need for standardized modeling frameworks, improved uncertainty treatment, and integrated multi-dimensional sustainability assessment to support reliable CE–LCA applications.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 系统综述了2019至2025年间61篇同行评审论文，通过分类分析生命周期评估方法、循环经济策略、系统边界及数字工具应用，揭示了动态与混合生命周期评估方法在建筑领域的兴起，同时指出结果对方法论假设高度敏感，而BIM、数字孪生等数字工具虽能增强数据可追溯性，但仍受互操作性挑战制约。

papers.title: An Integrated Cost–Quality–Time Project Control Framework for Saudi Infrastructure Projects
papers.abstract: The increased development of infrastructure in connection with Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 initiative makes it necessary to utilize advanced project control systems that could assist in reducing cost risks, guaranteeing quality, and minimizing schedule risks amid very complicated construction settings. Despite this, many projects still face numerous problems connected with disintegrated reporting, reactive decision-making, the absence of data integration, and insufficient deviation management. This review paper covers the latest literature from 2020 to 2025 about advanced project control systems for Saudi Arabia infrastructure projects with a special emphasis on earned value analysis, 4D/5D BIM, common data environments, digital twins, forecasting with artificial intelligence, lean production control, integrated quality management, and governance structures that promote alignment between field performance and top management decisions. The literature review is conducted through a systematic review process involving problem identification, study selection, coding, and comparison instead of a meta-analysis because the source material consists of theoretical papers, empirical research, case studies, and technology adoption studies. As a result of the review, it becomes evident that advanced project controls have to be used in an integrated managerial framework rather than as standalone software solutions. In terms of cost performance, there will be benefits when quantity, productivity, procurement, and change management information become consistently integrated into forecasting models and earned value analysis. As far as quality performance goes, the advantages will be achieved if inspection, non-conformance management, design coordination, and supplier management processes become automated and integrated into work planning. Concerning schedule performance, the improvements will come with the integration of look-ahead planning, progress sensing, predictive delay analysis, and fast decision-making procedures. It should be noted that the Saudi Arabian infrastructure projects face certain difficulties, such as being giga-projects, multi-stakeholder governance, dynamic scope development, localization, and different organizational maturity levels. This paper offers a model where advanced project controls function at five levels of integration: baseline determination, data gathering, analytics, decision-making, and corrective learning. . This model is referred to as the Integrated Cost–Quality–Time Control Framework for Saudi Infrastructure Projects (ICQTCF-SIP).It concludes that Saudi infrastructure clients and contractors should prioritize interoperable data environments, control-led governance, role clarity, and phased capability building to convert digital tools into measurable project outcomes.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对沙特阿拉伯基础设施项目，该综述论文系统分析了2020至2025年间先进项目控制系统的文献，提出需将挣值分析、4D/5D BIM、数字孪生等技术整合至统一管理框架中，而非作为独立软件应用。

papers.title: An Agentic AI and LLM-Based Framework for Probabilistic Cost Estimation from Fragmented BIM Data
papers.abstract: Building Information Modelling (BIM) has digitized construction, yet automated cost estimation still suffers from fragmented data and deterministic forecasts that ignore uncertainty. To address this gap, this study introduces a novel framework integrating agentic artificial intelligence (AI) with large language models (LLMs) to enable probabilistic cost estimation from disparate BIM data. The system employs four specialized collaborative agents operating via a shared memory module centered on an LLM with natural language understanding, code generation, and chain-of-thought reasoning. A prototype using GPT-4 Turbo, AutoGen, and Monte Carlo simulation was tested on three real-world structures. Compared to three baselines, the framework reduced processing time (4.2 vs. 18.5–68.0 min), manual interventions (0.8 vs. 9–14), and improved entity resolution accuracy (86.5% vs. 46–62%) with well-calibrated probabilistic forecasts, achieving 86.0% empirical coverage for nominal 90% prediction intervals (Prediction Interval Coverage Probability [PICP] = 86.0%, Prediction Interval Width [PIW] = 0.28; p < 0.01). Qualitative analysis confirmed effective semantic conflict resolution and actionable risk visualization via tornado diagrams. The framework tackles long-standing BIM estimation challenges by delivering probabilistic, transparent outputs. Future work includes digital twin integration, open-source LLM deployment, and during-construction forecasting.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种集成智能体AI与大语言模型的框架，从碎片化BIM数据实现概率成本估算。通过四个协作智能体、共享记忆模块及GPT-4 Turbo等工具，在三个真实测试中，处理时间降至4.2分钟，手动干预减至0.8次，实体解析准确率提升至86.5%，并生成校准良好的概率预测。

papers.title: Governing Data Integrity Risks in Digital Construction: A Qualitative Liability Framework for Generative AI Across the AEC Lifecycle and Smart City Operations
papers.abstract: Generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) can accelerate Building Information Modeling (BIM) and digital-twin workflows, but probabilistic outputs and manipulated inputs create data-integrity risks that conventional construction-software liability rules do not fully explain. This study develops a reproducible doctrinal and comparative-law method that maps the GenAI value chain onto the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) lifecycle. Its principal advantage over single-actor product or professional-negligence analyses is a qualitative proportional-liability assessment framework that evaluates technical control, foreseeability, verification capacity, causal contribution, and evidence preservation across developers, deployers, and professional users. The analysis identifies the following two recurrent pathways of harm: autonomous hallucination entering safety-relevant design information and adversarial or erroneous data entering digital-twin feedback loops. It also specifies syntax, semantic, cybersecurity, and human-verification controls linked to AEC information-management and quality standards. The framework is not an empirically calibrated formula and does not determine legally binding percentages. Instead, it provides a transparent decision aid for applying existing tort doctrines and regulatory duties to distributed GenAI-enabled construction workflows.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 采用比较法学方法，将生成式AI价值链映射至建筑全生命周期，构建定性比例责任评估框架，以应对BIM与数字孪生中的数据完整性风险。该框架通过评估开发者、部署者及专业用户的技术控制、可预见性等要素，为侵权法在分布式AI施工流程中的应用提供透明决策支持。

papers.title: A Design-Driven Full-Process Cost-Control Framework for EPC Projects Under Preliminary-Design Bill-of-Quantities Bidding
papers.abstract: With the increasing adoption of the Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) contracts in government-funded and large-scale infrastructure projects, bill-of-quantities bidding based on preliminary design has emerged as a new procurement approach. Although this approach improves early-stage investment control, it also imposes higher requirements on contractors’ cost-management capabilities. Based on whole-process cost-control theory, this study develops a design-driven full-process cost-control framework for EPC projects using a reclaimed water plant project in northwest China as a case study. The model comprises three layers: a design-driven decision-making layer, a whole-process cost-control layer, and a collaborative management support layer. It covers the key stages of bidding, design, procurement, construction, and final settlement, and integrates design, cost, and procurement management with Building Information Modeling (BIM) and dynamic monitoring based on Earned Value Management (EVM). The case results show that the model can effectively identify and control cost risks, promote the integration of design optimization and cost control, and improve cost management performance. The final settlement price was 0.93% below the contractual settlement ceiling and about 6.6% below the initial investment estimate. This study provides both theoretical support and practical guidance for enhancing full-process cost control in EPC projects under preliminary-design bill-of-quantities bidding.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对初步设计工程量清单招标的EPC项目，提出一个设计驱动的全过程成本控制框架，该框架包含设计驱动决策、全过程成本控制和协同管理支持三个层次，并整合BIM与挣值管理动态监控。案例应用表明，该框架能有效识别和控制成本风险，使最终结算价低于合同上限0.93%及初始投资估算约6.6%。

papers.title: The evolution of BIM work standards: current, best, and future practices from a BIM Manager's perspective
papers.abstract: This study analyzes the evolution of working standards in Building Information Modeling (BIM) and the redefinition of the role of the BIM Manager in the era of digital transformation. The starting point is the identification of a cognitive gap, i.e., the discrepancy between the exponential growth of data and the limitations of human perception. The conclusions are based on a narrative review of over 100 publications, with artificial intelligence models used in the content synthesis process. The analysis proves that implementing AI requires prior digital hygiene in accordance with the ISO 19650 standard and the conversion of data into a machine-readable format. Attention was also drawn to the phenomenon of the productivity paradox, showing that the automation of erroneous or inefficient processes only leads to the faster generation of defects. The paper outlines a vision of the future in which OpenBIM standards become the language of algorithm communication and AR technology ensures continuity of quality control. At the same time, the analysis emphasizes that the success of the transformation does not depend solely on technology, but on the soft and leadership skills necessary to overcome process resistance. The whole is crowned with a forecast of the evolution of the BIM Manager into a Knowledge Architect and transformation leader who oversees autonomous processes in a human-in-the-loop model.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 通过分析超过100篇文献并结合人工智能模型进行内容综合，揭示了BIM工作标准从当前实践到最佳实践的演进路径。研究指出，实施AI需先遵循ISO 19650标准进行数据数字化清洁，并强调OpenBIM标准将成为算法通信语言，而BIM经理将演变为知识架构师和转型领导者。

papers.title: Digital transformation in civil works sector: Regional insights and lessons from economies in transition
papers.abstract: The construction sector, historically characterized by operational fragmentation and chronically low productivity growth, is undergoing a paradigmatic shift propelled by the integration of digital technologies. Central to this evolution is Building Information Modeling (BIM), underpinned by synergistic technologies all contributing to enhanced interconnectivity, data centralization, and intelligent decision-making. In developing contexts, the diffusion of BIM remains constrained by a confluence of structural, institutional, and cultural impediments. Despite increasing policy attention, empirical research on BIM in Vietnam remains underdeveloped, especially with respect to field-based data collection. This study critically examines the predominant barriers impeding BIM adoption in the Vietnamese construction industry, contextualizing them within broader regional patterns observed in comparable developing economies. Key barriers are identified and analyzed through both global benchmarking and local stakeholder insights, offering a multi-scalar understanding of implementation bottlenecks. To address these systemic challenges, the study also proposes a suite of context-sensitive strategies, informed by successful interventions in more advanced BIM ecosystems but recalibrated for Vietnam’s socio-technical landscape. These strategies aim to bridge overarching national ambitions for digital transformation with the practical constraints and capacities of day-to-day implementation, thereby promoting a more coherent, data-oriented, and adaptive construction sector that can fully exploit BIM capabilities throughout all stages of the project lifecycle.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 通过全球基准与本地利益相关者洞察，识别并分析了越南建筑业采用BIM的主要障碍，并提出了适应其社会技术环境的策略，以弥合国家数字转型目标与日常实施限制之间的差距。

papers.title: SCIE EM OPENBIM: DO REGULAMENTO AO MODELO BIM VERIFICÁVEL
papers.abstract: A implementação de Automated Compliance Checking (ACC) em Segurança Contra Incêndios em Edifícios (SCIE) requer a transformação do raciocínio técnico e normativo em estruturas lógicas aptas a interpretação automática por máquinas. No projeto europeu ITEA4 FireBIM, está a ser desenvolvida uma metodologia com o intuito de traduzir regulamentos de SCIE em modelos processáveis, suportando verificação de conformidade em ambiente Building Information Modelling (BIM). A abordagem incide sobre a verificação regulamentar dos critérios de compartimentação, etapa determinante nas fases iniciais de projeto, devido ao impacto direto na organização espacial e viabilidade das soluções de arquitetura. A metodologia proposta estrutura o texto dos regulamentos em caminhos de decisão e mapas de processo, explicitando critérios, variáveis, exceções e condições de aplicabilidade. Esta formalização reduz ambiguidade interpretativa e estabelece a base para criação de machine-readable rules, essenciais ao ACC. Como consequência direta desta modelação lógica, são identificados requisitos de informação necessários à verificação da compartimentação em BIM, mapeando objetos, propriedades e atributos relevantes para formato Industry Foundation Classes (IFC). Para além disso, este mapeamento permite a articulação com mecanismos openBIM como Information Delivery Specification (IDS) e buildingSMART Data Dictionary (bSDD). Os resultados demonstram a viabilidade da transformação de regulamentos SCIE em lógica formal de decisão e respetivos requisitos de informação, evidenciando um caminho objetivo para a verificação automática de conformidade em fases preliminares de projeto e para a aproximação efetiva entre regulamentação técnica e modelos BIM.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对建筑防火安全（SCIE）的自动合规检查（ACC），提出一种将技术规范转化为机器可读逻辑规则的方法，通过决策路径和过程地图结构化法规文本，并映射至IFC格式的BIM对象与属性，实现设计初期阶段的自动合规验证。

papers.title: DE MODELOS BIM ESTÁTICOS A GÉMEOS DIGITAIS DINÂMICOS: UMA ABORDAGEM ABERTA E INTEROPERÁVEL
papers.abstract: A evolução recente do estado da arte em Building Information Modelling (BIM) e Gémeos Digitais evidencia a necessidade de soluções, que ultrapassem a visualização de modelos e integrem dados heterogéneos, ao longo do ciclo de vida dos ativos. Persistem limitações ligadas à fragmentação tecnológica, formatos proprietários, dificuldade em incorporar séries temporais e falta de mecanismos inteligentes de monitorização e validação. Este artigo apresenta uma plataforma de Gémeos Digitais que lida com estes desafios, numa abordagem aberta, modular e interoperável. Baseada em Industry Foundation Classes (IFC) e tecnologias open-source, a solução unifica modelos BIM, documentação multimodal e séries temporais de fontes externas. O backend propõe uma arquitetura que combina bases de dados relacionais, cache, armazenamento distribuído e configuração dinâmica de ligações a múltiplas bases de dados de séries temporais, permitindo integração escalável e flexível. Inclui um módulo inteligente com métodos de deteção de anomalias e monitorização da integridade dos fluxos de dados, reforçando a fiabilidade e automatizando diagnósticos. No frontend, a plataforma oferece um ambiente web unificado com visualização tridimensional de alta performance, navegação hierárquica e associação semântica entre elementos BIM, documentação e dados dinâmicos. As interfaces de gestão permitem configuração granular de propriedades, integrações, permissões e histórico, promovendo colaboração. Ao transformar modelos BIM estáticos em representações dinâmicas e continuamente informadas, a plataforma eleva a operacionalização dos Gémeos Digitais no setor da Arquitetura, Engenharia, Construção e Operação (AECO). O contributo do presente trabalho assenta na combinação de interoperabilidade aberta, integração multimodal e capacidades analíticas inteligentes, constituindo um avanço relevante para a gestão adaptativa de ativos construídos.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种基于IFC和开源技术的数字孪生平台，通过结合关系数据库、缓存、分布式存储及动态时序数据库连接，实现BIM模型、多模态文档与外部时序数据的统一集成，并内置异常检测与数据流完整性监控模块，将静态BIM模型转化为动态、持续更新的数字孪生体。

papers.title: INTEROPERABILIDADE EM MODELOS DE ANÁLISE ESTRUTURAL: ESTUDO DAS CONEXÕES ENTRE ELEMENTOS ANALÍTICOS
papers.abstract: O Building Information Modeling (BIM) permite atualização contínua dos modelos de análise estrutural, assegurando consistência entre os modelos físico e analítico. Na versão 4.3 do Industry Foundation Classes (IFC), a representação da análise estrutural é organizada em dois domínios principais: o IfcStructuralElementsDomain, que define os elementos físicos, e o IfcStructuralAnalysisDomain, destinado à representação do modelo analítico, que define as entidades que descrevem a conectividade entre membros e suas condições de ligação, como IfcStructuralConnection e suas subclasses. Este artigo tem como objetivo mapear e avaliar as entidades IFC relacionadas às conexões estruturais, investigando a comunicação do modelo analítico e sua aplicação na representação de ligações entre elementos. Este estudo inclui uma revisão bibliográfica e uma análise crítica da documentação oficial do IFC 4.3, abrangendo também o estudo da estrutura de dados contida em arquivos STEP. Diagramas e tabelas foram elaborados para facilitar a compreensão da estrutura do esquema. Conclui-se que a identificação das entidades e dos requisitos de informação realizada neste trabalho contribuem para o avanço da interoperabilidade entre ferramentas BIM, fortalecendo a precisão e a integração dos modelos físico e analítico.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 通过文献综述和IFC 4.3官方文档分析，研究了BIM中结构分析模型连接性的IFC实体映射与评估，揭示了IfcStructuralConnection等实体在物理与分析模型间互操作性的作用，为提升模型集成精度提供了数据支持。

papers.title: <b>ПРОБЛЕМЫ СТАНДАРТИЗАЦИИ BIM-ПРОЦЕССОВ В КОНТЕКСТЕ МЕЖДУНАРОДНЫХ НОРМ ISO 19650</b><b></b>
papers.abstract: This article investigates the standardisation challenges of Building Information Modelling (BIM) processes in the Republic of Kazakhstan within the context of ISO 19650, the international standard governing information management across the full lifecycle of built assets. The study is motivated by Kazakhstan's strategic commitment to digital transformation in construction under the Digital Kazakhstan programme. The methodology combines regulatory analysis of ISO 19650 and Kazakhstani legislation, structured expert interviews with representatives of 15 construction and design organisations, and a comparative BIM standardisation maturity analysis across four jurisdictions — Kazakhstan, the United Kingdom, Russia, and the UAE — using an original assessment matrix based on the BIM Maturity Index model. Content analysis of seven real EIR documents from Kazakhstani public-sector projects (2022–2025) was also conducted. The calculated BIM Standardisation Maturity Index places Kazakhstan at 1.75 out of 5.00 — an initial maturity level — behind the United Kingdom (4.95), the UAE (4.30), and Russia (2.90). Three barrier groups are identified: regulatory — BIM models lack legal status and no national standard exists; organisational — average EIR compliance with ISO 19650-2 was only 18%, and 93% of tender documents contained no BIM requirements; and technological — IFC format incompatibilities and the absence of a state CDE platform. A hybrid BIM ecosystem development strategy targeting a maturity index of 3.50 by 2030 is proposed, prioritising legal recognition of BIM models, adoption of a national standard based on ISO 19650, and establishment of a state CDE platform.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对哈萨克斯坦在ISO 19650国际标准背景下的BIM流程标准化挑战，通过法规分析、专家访谈和跨国成熟度比较，发现其BIM标准化成熟度指数仅为1.75（满分5.00），并识别出法规、组织和技术三类障碍，进而提出到2030年将成熟度提升至3.50的混合生态系统发展策略。

papers.title: VALIDAÇÃO AUTOMATIZADA DE ARQUIVOS IFC PARA GARANTIA DE QUALIDADE EM PROCESSOS OPENBIM
papers.abstract: A interoperabilidade entre plataformas BIM é um desafio recorrente em projetos que adotam o padrão aberto Industry Foundation Classes (IFC). A verificação manual da conformidade desses arquivos é um processo demorado, suscetível a erros e dependente de conhecimento técnico especializado. Este trabalho apresenta o desenvolvimento de um app de validação automatizada de arquivos IFC, concebido como um mecanismo de Garantia de Qualidade (QA) para modelos BIM. A solução foi baseada nas diretrizes e práticas da buildingSMART, com adaptações para uso interno, integrando-se ao fluxo de avaliação de modelos BIM. A arquitetura proposta combina Python, para leitura e interpretação semântica dos dados IFC, garantindo execução local e segurança das informações, sem dependência de servidores externos. Entre os diferenciais, destacam-se: validação semântica além da sintática, customização de regras matriz de informação do projeto, processamento consolidado e interface amigável para usuários não técnicos. Testes preliminares indicam potencial para reduzir em até 99% o tempo de análise, aumentar em 100% a cobertura da verificação de requisitos e diminuir falhas de interoperabilidade. A ferramenta contribui para a eficiência operacional, redução de custos e melhoria da qualidade dos modelos BIM, representando um avanço significativo para processos openBIM e certificação de conformidade em ambientes corporativos.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对BIM平台间互操作性挑战，开发了一种基于Python的IFC文件自动验证应用程序，通过语义与语法双重验证及可定制规则，实现质量保障。初步测试表明，该工具可将分析时间减少99%，验证覆盖率提升100%，并降低互操作性故障。

papers.title: Archetypen für deutsche Bürogebäude der Baujahre 1970, 1998 und 2026 - Detaillierte Dokumentation der architektonischen und bauphysikalischen Gebäudemodelle im IFC 4x3 Schema
papers.abstract: Im Forschungsprojekt Klassiqua wurden Büro-Archetypen für die Baualtersklassen 1970, 1998 und 2026 auf Grundlage statistischer Erhebungen entwickelt. Die Baujahre wurden mit dem Ziel gewählt, einen Altbau vor Inkrafttreten der ersten Wärmeschutzverordnung im Jahr 1977 zu zeigen, einen Zwischenbau und einen aktuellen Neubau.Die vorliegende Veröffentlichung beschreibt diese Archetypen sowie einbezogene Vorgaben und Referenzen bei der Ausarbeitung. Die Archetypen wurden unter Nutzung von Building Information Modeling (BIM) mit der openBIM-Software Blender Bonsai im IFC 4x3-Schema erstellt und stehen auch über den Projektkontext hinaus für die Nutzung durch Dritte zur Verfügung. Der Detaillierungsgrad entspricht einer Entwurfsplanung nach HOAI-Leistungsphase 3 (LOD 300). Die Bürogebäude-Archetypen dienen als Grundlage für energetische Jahressimulationen mit dem Ziel die Effekte von repräsentativen Smart Readiness Indicator (SRI)-Maßnahmenpaketen zu quantifizieren. Alle drei Archetypen basieren auf demselben architektonischen Grundmodell, welches mit baualterspezifischen Außenhüllen kombiniert wird. Den Baualtersklassen wurden überdies typische technische Anlagen für Erzeugung und Übergabe zugeordnet, welche nachfolgend auch als Techniksets bezeichnet werden. Diese werden im weiteren Projektverlauf in der Simulation abgebildet und sind nicht in den IFC-Modellen enthalten.
papers.venue: Zenodo (CERN European Organization for Nuclear Research)
papers.ai_summary_zh: 在Klassiqua研究项目中，基于统计调查开发了1970、1998和2026年建成的德国办公建筑原型，利用Blender Bonsai软件在IFC 4x3模式下创建了BIM模型，详细记录了建筑与物理模型，为量化智能就绪指标措施包的年度能耗模拟提供基础。

papers.title: ATIVOS MAIS EFICIENTES COM GÉMEOS DIGITAIS: INTEGRAÇÃO BIM E OPERAÇÃO
papers.abstract: A crescente maturidade da metodologia Building Information Modeling (BIM) em Portugal tem impulsionado a sua extensão para além das fases de projeto e construção, abrangendo a operação e gestão de ativos. No entanto, a transição entre o modelo BIM estático e a gestão operacional baseada em dados em tempo real continua a representar um desafio técnico e organizacional. Este artigo apresenta o desenvolvimento e validação de um gémeo digital numa Estação de Tratamento de Águas Residuais (ETAR), integrando modelo BIM, documentação técnica e dados operacionais provenientes do ativo e de laboratório. A solução, implementada através da plataforma Q. BIMConnect, permitiu consolidar 62 pontos de dados, gerar histórico operacional de cinco meses e suportar análises técnicas com impacto direto na eficiência energética e estabilidade do processo. Os resultados demonstram o potencial da abordagem na identificação de oportunidades de poupança energética estimadas em cerca de 3 970 €/mês apenas na fase piloto. O caso de estudo evidencia a importância da interoperabilidade OpenBIM (IFC) e da governação de dados como fatores críticos para a escalabilidade da solução.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对BIM模型向实时运维管理过渡的技术挑战，本研究在污水处理厂开发并验证了集成BIM模型、技术文档与运营数据的数字孪生方案，通过Q. BIMConnect平台整合62个数据点并生成五个月运营历史，在试点阶段实现约3970欧元/月的节能潜力，证实了OpenBIM（IFC）互操作性与数据治理对方案可扩展性的关键作用。

papers.title: FROM BIM TO INTERACTIVE DIGITAL TWIN: A WORKFLOW FOR INTEGRATING VIRTUAL AND PHYSICAL ENVIRONMENTS
papers.abstract: The fragmentation among Building Information Models (BIM), IoT sensor networks, and facility management interfaces severely limits the operational value of digital construction investments. The architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) sector requires practical workflows that extend the use of BIM beyond design and construction into facility operations. This paper presents a validated open-source methodology for transforming static IFC models into interactive augmented virtual reality (VR) digital twins using Unreal Engine 5.5. The approach has been implemented through a 4-level university building case study. The proposed system combines IFC building geometry with real-time HOBO sensor data (temperature, humidity, and CO2) using a three-tier navigation architecture that bridges 2D floor plans and 3D spatial exploration. Using Datasmith for model processing and Blueprint visual scripting for interaction logic, the workflow supports both desktop and VR deployment modes without requiring programming expertise. Performance validation demonstrates sub-100 ms response times for all interactions, teleportation for predefined spaces, and consistent 120 FPS desktop/90-120 FPS VR operation while processing live sensor streams. The multi-modal interface design enables progressive complexity adoption, allowing facility managers to transition from familiar 2D interfaces to immersive 3D environments at their own pace. This democratized approach uses freely available tools to make digital twin technology accessible to educational institutions and small-to-medium enterprises previously excluded by proprietary platform costs. The validated workflow directly provides practical implementation patterns for BIM-to-digital-twin transformation. This solution serves AEC researchers and academic institutions seeking an open, extensible framework for experimental digital twin development, immersive visualization research, and real-time BIM–IoT integration studies without reliance on proprietary platforms.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种将静态IFC模型转化为交互式增强虚拟现实数字孪生的开源方法，通过Unreal Engine 5.5整合BIM几何与实时传感器数据，并在四层大学建筑案例中验证了亚100毫秒响应与高帧率性能。

papers.title: Comparative Evaluation of Pix4Dmapper and Agisoft Metashape for UAS-Based 3D Reconstruction toward Building Information Modeling (BIM)
papers.abstract: Uncrewed Aerial Systems (UAS) offer an efficient alternative to traditional building documentation by enabling rapid, high-resolution data collection for Building Information Modeling (BIM). This study presents a comparative evaluation of Pix4Dmapper and Agisoft Metashape using seven UAS datasets of Kaplan Hall collected with a DJI Mavic 3T. The analysis focuses on accuracy, model quality, processing efficiency, and usability to understand how each platform supports building-scale photogrammetric reconstruction. Results show that Pix4Dmapper provides faster and more automated processing with lower reprojection errors, making it suitable for routine engineering workflows, while Agisoft Metashape generates denser point clouds and more detailed meshes, offering advantages for high-fidelity modeling. Both tools demonstrated compatibility with Building Information Modeling (BIM) environments, supporting their use in Scan-to-BIM applications. Overall, the findings highlight tradeoffs between speed and detail and offer guidance for selecting photogrammetry platforms for UAS-enabled building modeling and digital documentation.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 基于七组无人机数据集，比较评估Pix4Dmapper与Agisoft Metashape在建筑信息模型三维重建中的性能。Pix4Dmapper处理更快、自动化更高且重投影误差更低，而Agisoft Metashape生成更密集点云和更精细网格，两者均支持扫描到BIM应用。

papers.title: INTEROPERABILITY BETWEEN BIM AND ROBOTIC FABRICATION PROCESSES: A CASE STUDY APPLIED TO GENERATIVE FACADE DESIGN
papers.abstract: This paper investigates the interoperability between Building Information Modelling (BIM) environments and robotic fabrication (RF) workflows applied to generative 3D concrete printing. The study proposes a framework that starts with parametric modelling defined in a BIM environment, incorporates parametric code to structure and export metadata, and translates this information into robot code for extrusion-based fabrication. The workflow integrates geometry, dimensional parameters (height and width), volumetric data, and IFC metadata, automatically organizing and exporting them to ensure consistency between the digital model and the physical prototype. Two BIM-based export strategies were developed and compared through IFC, highlighting differences in geometric fidelity and data representation. The results indicate that, although BIM platforms present limitations in handling complex geometry, they remain effective for managing fabrication-related parameters. A scaled prototype was produced to validate the full process. The transition from full-scale geometry to a reduced-scale prototype required only minor adjustments to the base geometry, confirming the robustness of the workflow. Issues observed during fabrication were mainly related to extrusion speed, material plasticity, and design choices, rather than to interoperability. Overall, the study demonstrates that BIM workflows can be successfully integrated with RF when geometric complexity and metadata interoperability are properly managed.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 本研究探讨了建筑信息模型（BIM）与机器人制造（RF）工作流之间的互操作性，以生成式3D混凝土打印为案例，提出了一种从BIM环境参数化建模到机器人代码导出的框架。通过对比两种基于BIM的IFC导出策略，验证了该流程在管理几何与元数据一致性上的有效性，并指出BIM平台虽在处理复杂几何方面存在局限，但通过适当管理仍可与RF成功集成。

papers.title: O OPENBIM NO DESIGN and BUILD: DA VISÃO À IMPLEMENTAÇÃO
papers.abstract: A adoção do Building Information Modelling (BIM) de forma transversal é dificultada pela descontinuidade entre os modelos desenvolvidos em fase de projeto e as necessidades específicas das equipas na frente de obra. Esta lacuna obriga os empreiteiros a reformular os modelos, o que limita a integração eficaz desta metodologia em formato aberto ao longo do processo. Este artigo descreve a visão estratégica do openBIM numa empresa de Design and Build, na qual a integração dos processos de conceção e construção oferece uma excelente oportunidade para superar essas barreiras. É apresentado um fluxograma que capitaliza a flexibilidade do software proprietário na fase de conceção dos projetos, estabelecendo, simultaneamente, uma troca de informações rigorosa e controlada, por meio de formatos abertos, na transição para a fase de obra. A definição dos requisitos de produção e de partilha de informação garante um fluxo de trabalho otimizado, potenciando a interoperabilidade na gestão do projeto e da obra, bem como na entrega do ativo digital ao cliente. Por fim, é partilhado o estado de implementação desta metodologia, as lições aprendidas e as perspetivas futuras, concluindo-se que esta abordagem controlada do openBIM é crucial para maximizar a eficiência e estabelecer um ciclo de vida do ativo digital robusto e auditável.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对设计与施工阶段模型不连续导致openBIM整合困难的问题，提出在Design and Build公司中采用战略愿景，通过流程图结合专有软件灵活性与开放格式的严格信息交换，优化工作流并提升互操作性，同时分享了实施状态与经验教训。

papers.title: USO DE IDS: AVALIAÇÃO DE FLUXO DE VERIFICAÇÃO DE CONFORMIDADE COM PYTHON E BONSAI
papers.abstract: A crescente adoção do Industry Foundation Classes (IFC) impulsiona a interoperabilidade e a troca estruturada de informação ao longo do ciclo de vida dos projetos. Neste ecossistema openBIM, definiu-se o Information Delivery Specification (IDS), formato padronizado e legível por máquina (machine-readable), que permite verificar requisitos de informação, assegurando a consistência e a conformidade dos modelos IFC face aos requisitos de informação do projeto. Este artigo apresenta abordagens na definição, geração e verificação com IDS, comparando casos práticos de fluxos complementares em projeto da TPF. Na abordagem desenvolvida pela TPF Engenharia, descreve-se um processo baseado em requisitos tabulares, convertidos para IDS via script Python que gera um XML conforme a especificação IDS. A verificação é realizada com o módulo do Bonsai BIM (Blender), permitindo avaliar automaticamente a conformidade dos modelos IFC e gerar relatórios. Como termo de comparação, são analisadas outras soluções disponíveis no ecossistema openBIM, nomeadamente o módulo usBIM.IDSeditor e a plataforma BIMCollab. Estas abordagens permitem a definição e verificação de requisitos de informação por meio de interfaces gráficas, com diferentes níveis de integração entre a criação do IDS e a verificação de informação nos modelos IFC. A comparação das várias perspetivas evidencia a versatilidade do IDS na gestão de requisitos BIM, oferecendo contributos práticos para a sua implementação em ambientes openBIM.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 通过Python脚本将表格化需求转换为符合IDS规范的XML文件，并利用Bonsai BIM模块自动验证IFC模型合规性，同时对比usBIM.IDSeditor和BIMCollab等工具，展示了IDS在openBIM生态中定义与核查信息需求的灵活性与实用性。

papers.title: DIGITALIZAÇÃO PARAMÉTRICA DE UM PRODUTO INOVADOR (UTS) COM FAMÍLIAS, PDT E ROTINAS DYNAMO
papers.abstract: A crescente necessidade de adoção de sistemas construtivos industrializados em madeira, como alternativa a soluções tradicionais em betão armado, coloca desafios relevantes à sua representação consistente em ambientes Building Information Modelling (BIM). Neste contexto, o artigo apresenta uma abordagem metodológica para a digitalização BIM de um sistema de lajes em madeira de elevada complexidade, utilizando o Universal Timber Slab (UTS) como caso de estudo. O trabalho descreve o desenvolvimento de um conjunto integrado de famílias BIM paramétricas, concebidas para traduzir a lógica construtiva do sistema, estruturadas segundo princípios openBIM e suportadas por um Product Data Template (PDT) conforme as normas EN ISO 23386 e EN ISO 23387. O PDT permite estruturar e normalizar a informação técnica, material e de ciclo de vida associada aos elementos, assegurando consistência semântica e interoperabilidade entre plataformas. Para apoiar a correta utilização das famílias e reduzir tarefas repetitivas de modelação, foram implementadas rotinas Dynamo que testam a segmentação da laje, identificando os apoios e estabelecendo a ligação entre os diferentes componentes do sistema. Estes automatismos contribuem para a coerência do modelo em fase de projeto e para a disponibilização de informação estruturada para fases posteriores. O artigo discute, através da articulação entre PDT, famílias paramétricas e automação de que forma é possível desenvolver um ecossistema BIM coerente para a representação de sistemas construtivos industrializados e complexos. A abordagem apresentada é enquadrada enquanto metodologia transferível para a modelação BIM de produtos construtivos com características semelhantes no setor da Arquitetura, Engenharia, Construção e Operações (AECO).
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种针对复杂木结构楼板系统（UTS）的BIM参数化数字化方法，通过开发集成参数化族、遵循EN ISO 23386/23387标准的产品数据模板（PDT）以及Dynamo自动化例程，确保模型语义一致性与跨平台互操作性。

papers.title: AUGMENTED REALITY FOR MODULAR CONSTRUCTION: A BIM DRIVEN FACTORY TO SITE WORKFLOW
papers.abstract: The research presents ARsembly, an open source framework that bridges Building Information Modeling (BIM) and Augmented Reality (AR) within the Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations (AECO) sector. The study addresses a persistent gap between traditional BIM AR proposals and their practical implementation in real-world workflows. Through the development of two interlinked frameworks, BIM AR integration and User Interface (UI), the work demonstrates a feasible, replicable approach for interactive BIM visualization and decision support in AR environments. The BIM AR framework tackles key technical challenges such as model preparation, file transformation, runtime rendering, and device optimization, establishing a GLTF-based runtime pipeline compatible with free and open source tools. In parallel, the UI framework defines a modular structure that separates general functionality from AR-specific interaction, focusing on usability, cognitive clarity, and multidisciplinary adaptability. Together, these frameworks converge in ARsembly, a prototype developed in Unity with C#, Docker, and Python scripting to enable real-time BIM model visualization, metadata management, and AR-based interaction. Results validate the feasibility of the proposed system through iterative testing, benchmarking, and partial implementation. The research concludes that BIM AR integration must be both technically grounded and user-centered, with flexibility and accessibility as key enablers of adoption in the AECO industry. ARsembly thus contributes a scalable model for open, interoperable, and human centric digital workflows supporting the ongoing transformation of construction processes toward smarter and data-driven environments.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出ARsembly开源框架，通过开发BIM-AR集成与用户界面两个子框架，解决建筑信息模型与增强现实在AECO领域实际工作流中的整合难题，并基于GLTF运行时管线与Unity原型验证了交互式可视化与决策支持的可行性。

papers.title: AN OPEN-SOURCE APPROACH FOR MULTI-SENSOR SCAN-TO-BIM WORKFLOWS
papers.abstract: Open-source methodologies promote broader access to knowledge, tools, and technological development by supporting reproducible and community-driven advancements. By integrating digital surveying into these workflows, it is possible to reduce the existing dependency of the construction field on proprietary software solutions. For this purpose, this study aims to develop an open-source Scan-to-BIM approach, based on multi-sensor surveying. Scan-to-BIM refers to the process of using captured spatial data to create accurate as-built/as-is BIM models, facilitating tasks such as renovation and maintenance throughout the infrastructure lifecycle. This study employs the open-source software Blender, paired with the Bonsai add-on, as the central platform for compiling data gathered from aerial and handheld surveys conducted at FEUP. The case study integrates three major sensing technologies to capture both exterior and interior data: UAV-based photogrammetry and thermography (which enabled an additional Image-to-BIM process), as well as LiDAR scanning via an iOS device. The results prove that an affordable open-source Scan-to-BIM workflow is fully achievable, thus enabling smaller entities to access high-quality 3D documentation. Photogrammetry achieved millimetric-level accuracy relative to the real building, while the LiDAR surveys required little to no scale adjustment to match the results of both sensors, indicating the successful integration of multiple data sources within the open-source workflow. Finally, Blender, along with the Bonsai add-on, proved to be a versatile platform capable of efficiently managing complex Scan-to-BIM processes. Overall, the study demonstrates that open-source tools can support advanced BIM tasks; therefore, offering a practical and cost-effective alternative to proprietary software solutions.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 提出一种基于多传感器测量的开源扫描到BIM工作流，以Blender和Bonsai插件为核心平台，集成无人机摄影测量、热成像与iOS设备激光雷达数据。案例验证了该方法可实现毫米级精度，并有效整合多源数据，为小型实体提供低成本的高质量三维文档方案。

papers.title: OTIMIZAÇÃO DO ALINHAMENTO BIM–SCAN PARA AVALIAÇÃO DE PROGRESSO DA CONSTRUÇÃO
papers.abstract: A monitorização automática do progresso da construção enfrenta desafios de alinhamento entre os dados as-is de obra e a intenção de projeto representada em modelos de Building Information Modelling (BIM), bem como limitações computacionais. Muitos métodos recentes recorrem a redes profundas complexas, com elevado custo de processamento e memória, dificultando a inferência em tempo quase real em dispositivos com recursos limitados, como robots, drones e plataformas móveis. Torna-se, assim, necessário desenvolver metodologias capazes de integrar informação geométrica e semântica de forma integrada, mas com requisitos computacionais reduzidos e boa escalabilidade. Na prática, os dados recolhidos em obra são frequentemente ruidosos, incompletos e afetados por oclusões e montagens parciais, limitando a fiabilidade das abordagens existentes. Este trabalho explora uma metodologia para melhorar a robustez e a interpretabilidade dos processos de alinhamento BIM–scan e de estimativa de progresso. A abordagem enfatiza a integração da estrutura semântica com o raciocínio geométrico, reforçando a correspondência entre elementos digitalizados e modelados e permitindo uma quantificação mais consistente do progresso ao longo das fases de construção. Em particular, a estruturação semântica orienta o alinhamento, em vez de tratar segmentação e registo como etapas isoladas. Adicionalmente, propõe-se uma estratégia computacional leve e eficiente, adequada à monitorização quase em tempo real e alinhada com a digitalização do ambiente construído. Ao privilegiar a otimização metodológica em detrimento da complexidade arquitetónica, pretende-se avançar na automação mantendo a aplicabilidade prática em obra. O resultado esperado é uma base mais fiável, eficiente e escalável para o acompanhamento do progresso da construção, em fluxos de trabalho suportados em BIM.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 针对BIM扫描与施工进度评估中的对齐挑战，提出一种融合语义结构与几何推理的轻量化方法，通过优化语义引导的对齐流程而非孤立处理分割与配准，在降低计算需求的同时提升鲁棒性和可解释性，适用于机器人、无人机等资源受限设备的近实时监测。

papers.title: AVALIAÇÃO DAS REPRESENTAÇÕES GEOMÉTRICAS IFC NO CONTEXTO DE USD E IFC5
papers.abstract: Este artigo analisa a compatibilidade entre as representações geométricas do esquema IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) e o Universal Scene Description (USD), com o intuito de fundamentar o desenvolvimento do futuro IFC5. A investigação salienta que, enquanto o IFC privilegia o rigor semântico e a parametrização procedimental, o USD foca-se na eficiência da visualização em tempo real. A conversão de IFC para USD resulta frequentemente no "achatamento" de geometrias complexas em malhas estáticas, provocando a perda de inteligência paramétrica e um aumento significativo no tamanho dos ficheiros. Testes práticos indicam que esta limitação restringe o USD a funções consultivas ou visuais. Para o IFC5, recomenda-se a hibridização de esquemas procedimentais e declarativos, a padronização de metadados personalizados no USD e o desacoplamento entre semântica e representação geométrica. Conclui-se que o desafio reside em integrar o desempenho do USD sem sacrificar o rigor técnico essencial para a engenharia e construção.
papers.ai_summary_zh: 该研究分析了IFC几何表示与通用场景描述（USD）的兼容性，通过实践测试发现IFC向USD转换会导致参数智能丢失和文件体积增大，为未来IFC5开发提出了混合程序化与声明式方案、标准化USD元数据等建议。

papers.title: O PAPEL DO BIM NO DESENVOLVIMENTO DE ESPAÇOS COMUNS DE DADOS PARA A CONSTRUÇÃO
papers.abstract: Na era da digitalização e da inteligência artificial em que vivemos, os dados são reconhecidamente o novo ouro. Reconhecendo a sua importância, a estratégia Europeia para os dados foi lançada em 2020 com vista a delinear a forma como a Europa se devia preparar para tirar partido desta sua riqueza. Uma das peças fundamentais neste processo seriam os Espaços Comuns de Dados, existindo desde então várias iniciativas nesse sentido em diferentes setores. Apesar da maioria dos Espaços de Dados se encontrar em desenvolvimento, já existem alguns em operação, como os da Saúde e Mobilidade. Nos últimos anos, impulsionada pela crescente digitalização em curso, começou a ser discutida a criação de Espaços de Dados no setor da construção.
